message:
feat(agent-sweep): Add file size/binary validation, fix arg parsing

Code review fixes:
- Fix pending_global_args to include agent-sweep (--json, --dry-run,
  --resume, etc. now work regardless of position)
- Add --json to pending_global_args pattern for consistency

New file validation functions:
- agent_sweep_is_positive_int: Validate positive integers
- set_agent_sweep_max_file_mb/bytes: Sync MB and bytes values
- apply_agent_sweep_max_file_limit: Apply config values
- is_file_too_large: Check file size against limits
- is_binary_file: Detect binary vs text files
- is_binary_allowed: Honor allow patterns for binaries

Configuration improvements:
- Add max_file_mb as preferred config option (clearer than bytes)
- Add --max-file-mb CLI override
- Add AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS_DEFAULT for common binaries
- Rename log_activity_snapshot parameter for clarity

Test improvements:
- Add missing log stubs to artifact capture tests
- Fix run_test to preserve globals (not in subshell)
- Add tests for is_file_too_large, is_binary_file, is_binary_allowed

 {"id":"bd-51fm","title":"Implement repo_preflight_check()","description":"# Repository Preflight Checks

## Parent Epic: bd-cpxq (Preflight Safety Checks)

## Purpose
Validate repo is in safe state before invoking agent.

## Implementation

```bash
repo_preflight_check() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON=""

    # Is it a git repo?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="not_a_git_repo"
        return 1
    fi

    # Git identity configured? (ADDED: prevents commit failures)
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.email 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_email_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.name 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_name_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi

    # Shallow clone? (ADDED: some operations may fail)
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/shallow" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="shallow_clone"
        return 1
    fi

    # Dirty submodules? (ADDED: complex state)
    if git -C "$repo_path" submodule status 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^+'; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="dirty_submodules"
        return 1
    fi

    # Rebase in progress?
    if [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-apply" ]] || [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-merge" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="rebase_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Merge in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/MERGE_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="merge_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cherry-pick in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="cherry_pick_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Detached HEAD?
    local branch
    branch=$(git -C "$repo_path" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$branch" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="detached_HEAD"
        return 1
    fi

    # Has upstream?
    local upstream
    upstream=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --abbrev-ref "@{u}" 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$upstream" ]] && [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_PUSH_STRATEGY:-push}" != "none" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="no_upstream_branch"
        return 1
    fi

    # Diverged from upstream?
    if [[ -n "$upstream" ]]; then
        local ahead behind
        read -r ahead behind < <(git -C "$repo_path" rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ "$ahead" -gt 0 ]] && [[ "$behind" -gt 0 ]]; then
            PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diverged_from_upstream"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Unmerged paths?
    if git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --unmerged 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="unmerged_paths"
        return 1
    fi

    # git diff --check clean?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" diff --check &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diff_check_failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Too many untracked files?
    local untracked_count
    untracked_count=$(git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    if [[ "$untracked_count" -gt "${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_UNTRACKED:-1000}" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="too_many_untracked_files"
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}
```

## Skip Reason to User Action Mapping

| Reason | User Action |
|--------|-------------|
| git_email_not_configured | Run: git config user.email "you@example.com" |
| git_name_not_configured | Run: git config user.name "Your Name" |
| shallow_clone | Run: git fetch --unshallow |
| dirty_submodules | Fix submodule state first |
| rebase_in_progress | Complete or abort rebase |
| merge_in_progress | Complete or abort merge |
| detached_HEAD | Switch to a branch |
| no_upstream_branch | Set upstream tracking branch |
| diverged_from_upstream | Pull and rebase first |
| too_many_untracked_files | Review .gitignore, clean untracked |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 preflight conditions checked
- [ ] Skip reasons are human-readable
- [ ] User action mapping covers all skip reasons
- [ ] Tests cover all skip scenarios

 {"id":"bd-52ah","title":"Update install.sh with ntm integration","description":"# Installer Integration for ntm

## Parent Epic: bd-3ov0 (Installer Integration)

## Changes to install.sh

### 1. Add ntm Detection
```bash
check_ntm_installed() {
    command -v ntm &>/dev/null
}

get_ntm_version() {
    ntm --version 2>/dev/null | head -1
}
```

### 2. Add Interactive Prompt
```bash
prompt_ntm_install() {
    [[ "${RU_NON_INTERACTIVE:-}" == "1" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "yes" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "no" ]] && return 1
    
    cat << EOF
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│          Enable ntm integration?                            │
│                                                             │
│   Enables: ru agent-sweep                                   │
│   Provides: AI-assisted commit and release automation       │
│                                                             │
│   [Y] Yes (recommended)                                     │
│   [n] No, skip for now                                      │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
EOF
    read -r response
    [[ "$response" =~ ^[yY]$ ]] || [[ -z "$response" ]]
}
```

### 3. Add ntm Installation
```bash
install_ntm() {
    if check_ntm_installed; then
        log_info "ntm already installed: $(get_ntm_version)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_step "Installing ntm..."
    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash
}
```

### 4. Integration Flow
After ru install:
1. Check tmux (warn if missing)
2. Prompt for ntm (respects env vars)
3. Install ntm if requested
4. Verify installation

### Environment Variables
- RU_INSTALL_NTM=yes: Auto-install ntm
- RU_INSTALL_NTM=no: Skip ntm
- RU_NON_INTERACTIVE=1: Install everything","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:56:50.348655008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:06:13.576140339-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:06:13.576140339-05:00","close_reason":"Added ntm integration to install.sh with detection, prompt, and installation functions"}

## Parent Epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails & Validation)

## Purpose
Prevent committing large files or unexpected binaries.

## File Size Limit

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB="${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB:-10}"

is_file_too_large() {
    local file="$1"
    local max_bytes=$((AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB * 1024 * 1024))
    local size
    
    # Cross-platform stat
    size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    [[ "$size" -gt "$max_bytes" ]]
}
```

## Binary Detection

```bash
is_binary_file() {
    local file="$1"
    # file command returns "... text" for text files
    \! file -b "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qiE "(text|script|json|xml|empty)"
}
```

## Integration

Check during validate_commit_plan():
1. For each file in plan
2. If file exists and is too large: block
3. If file is binary without explicit allow: block

## Configuration

- --max-file-mb=N: Override default 10MB limit
- AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS: Patterns for allowed binaries

## Allowed Binary Patterns

Some binaries are expected (icons, fonts):
```
*.png
*.jpg
*.gif
*.ico
*.woff
*.woff2
```

 {"id":"bd-5jph","title":"Implement work item priority scoring algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement Work Item Priority Scoring Algorithm

## Purpose
Calculate priority scores for individual issues and PRs (not repos) to enable intelligent work ordering. Higher scores = review first.

## Background: Why Item-Level Scoring?
- Repo-level scoring is too coarse
- A repo with 100 issues shouldn't dominate queue
- Security bugs in small repos should surface
- Enables filtering: --priority=high skips LOW items

## Score Components

### Component 1: Type Importance (0-20 points)
```bash
# PRs indicate someone invested effort
if [[ "$item_type" == "pr" ]]; then
    score=$((score + 20))
    # Draft PRs get penalized
    [[ "$is_draft" == "true" ]] && score=$((score - 15))
else
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 2: Label-Based Priority (0-50 points)
```bash
if echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'security|critical'; then
    score=$((score + 50))
elif echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'bug|urgent'; then
    score=$((score + 30))
elif echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'enhancement|feature'; then
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 3: Age Factor (0-50 points for bugs, penalty for old features)
```bash
age_days=$(days_since_timestamp "$created_at")

# Bug/security items: older = more urgent (they're waiting!)
if echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'bug|security|critical'; then
    if [[ $age_days -gt 60 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 50))
    elif [[ $age_days -gt 30 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 30))
    elif [[ $age_days -gt 14 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 15))
    fi
else
    # Feature requests: very old ones may be stale, don't prioritize
    if [[ $age_days -gt 180 ]]; then
        score=$((score - 10))
    fi
fi
```

### Component 4: Recency Bonus (0-15 points)
```bash
# Recently updated items have active engagement
updated_days=$(days_since_timestamp "$updated_at")
if [[ $updated_days -lt 3 ]]; then
    score=$((score + 15))
elif [[ $updated_days -lt 7 ]]; then
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 5: Staleness Penalty (-20 points)
```bash
# Already reviewed items should be deprioritized
local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
if item_recently_reviewed "$item_key"; then
    score=$((score - 20))
fi
```

## Helper Functions

### days_since_timestamp()
```bash
days_since_timestamp() {
    local ts="$1"
    local now=$(date +%s)
    local then
    # Handle both Linux and macOS date formats
    then=$(date -d "$ts" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
           date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "$ts" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
           echo "$now")
    echo $(( (now - then) / 86400 ))
}
```

### item_recently_reviewed()
```bash
item_recently_reviewed() {
    local item_key="$1"
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    [[ ! -f "$state_file" ]] && return 1
    
    local last_review
    last_review=$(jq -r --arg key "$item_key" '.items[$key].last_review // ""' "$state_file")
    
    [[ -z "$last_review" ]] && return 1
    
    local days_since
    days_since=$(days_since_timestamp "$last_review")
    
    [[ $days_since -lt ${REVIEW_SKIP_DAYS:-7} ]]
}
```

### get_priority_level()
```bash
get_priority_level() {
    local score="$1"
    if [[ $score -ge 150 ]]; then echo "CRITICAL"
    elif [[ $score -ge 100 ]]; then echo "HIGH"
    elif [[ $score -ge 50 ]]; then echo "NORMAL"
    else echo "LOW"
    fi
}
```

### passes_priority_threshold()
```bash
passes_priority_threshold() {
    local level="$1"
    local threshold="$2"
    
    case "$threshold" in
        all) return 0 ;;
        normal) [[ "$level" != "LOW" ]] ;;
        high) [[ "$level" == "HIGH" || "$level" == "CRITICAL" ]] ;;
        critical) [[ "$level" == "CRITICAL" ]] ;;
    esac
}
```

## Priority Levels

| Score Range | Level | Color | Behavior |
|-------------|-------|-------|----------|
| 150+ | CRITICAL | Red | Process immediately |
| 100-149 | HIGH | Orange | First batch |
| 50-99 | NORMAL | Yellow | When capacity available |
| 0-49 | LOW | Gray | Last, or skip with --skip-low |

## Example Scores
- Security bug, 45 days old: 10 + 50 + 30 = 90 (HIGH)
- New PR with bug label: 20 + 30 + 15 = 65 (NORMAL)
- Draft PR, enhancement: 20 - 15 + 10 = 15 (LOW)
- Critical issue, 90 days: 10 + 50 + 50 = 110 (HIGH)

## Testing
- Unit tests for each component
- Edge cases: no labels, very old items, draft PRs
- Verify staleness penalty applies correctly
- Verify threshold filtering works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] calculate_item_priority_score() returns consistent scores
- [ ] All 5 components contribute appropriately
- [ ] Priority levels map correctly to score ranges
- [ ] Threshold filtering works for all levels

 {"id":"bd-5ljl","title":"Update CI workflow for agent-sweep tests","description":"# CI Workflow Update for agent-sweep Tests

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Purpose
Add agent-sweep tests to the CI workflow so tests run automatically on PRs.

## Changes to .github/workflows/ci.yml

### Add New Test Jobs

```yaml
agent-sweep-unit-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install test dependencies
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install -y jq
    - name: Run ntm driver unit tests
      run: scripts/test_unit_ntm_driver.sh
    - name: Run security guardrail tests
      run: scripts/test_security_guardrails.sh
    - name: Run preflight check tests
      run: scripts/test_preflight_checks.sh
    - name: Run plan validation tests
      run: scripts/test_plan_validation.sh
    - name: Run state management tests
      run: scripts/test_state_management.sh

agent-sweep-e2e-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install test dependencies
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install -y jq tmux
    - name: Setup mock ntm
      run: |
        mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
        cp scripts/test_bin/ntm ~/.local/bin/
        chmod +x ~/.local/bin/ntm
        export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
    - name: Run E2E tests with mock
      run: scripts/test_e2e_agent_sweep.sh
```

### Add Test Matrix

- ubuntu-latest
- macos-latest (if applicable)

### Integration with Existing Jobs

- Use job dependencies to ensure ShellCheck passes first

- [ ] E2E tests run with mock ntm
- [ ] Tests pass on ubuntu-latest
- [ ] ShellCheck job runs before test jobs
- [ ] Test failures block PR merge
- [ ] Test output is visible in GitHub Actions logs

 {"id":"bd-6nuc","title":"Implement per-repo configuration loading","description":"Implements load_repo_agent_config() to support per-repository agent-sweep customization.

## Background

The NTM integration plan specifies support for per-repo .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json files that can override default agent-sweep behavior. This enables repository maintainers to:
- Enable/disable agent sweeps for their repo
- Customize file denylist patterns
- Set repo-specific size limits
- Configure custom phase prompts or skip certain phases
- Define pre/post hooks

## Function to Implement

### load_repo_agent_config()

Purpose: Load and merge per-repo configuration with global defaults.

Location: ~/.config/ru/agent-sweep.yml (global) merged with repo/.ru-agent.yml

Config Schema:
```yaml
# Enable/disable for this repo
enabled: true

# Override file patterns to deny
file_denylist_extra:
  - "*.generated.ts"
  - "vendor/*"

# Override file size limit (bytes)
max_file_size: 5242880  # 5MB

# Skip phases
skip_phases:
  - release  # Skip phase 3 for this repo

# Custom understanding context
extra_context: |
  This repo uses a monorepo structure.
  Database migrations are in db/migrations/.

# Hooks (run in repo context)
pre_sweep_hook: "./scripts/pre-agent.sh"
post_sweep_hook: "./scripts/post-agent.sh"
```

Implementation Steps:
1. Check for .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json in repo root
2. Parse YAML/JSON using portable approach (yq -> python3 -> simple parser)
3. Merge with global config (repo overrides global)
4. Validate configuration values
5. Return merged config as associative array or temp file

## Portable YAML Parsing

Since this is pure Bash, implement layered fallback:
1. yq (if available) - fastest, most reliable
2. python3 -c "import yaml..." (common fallback)
3. Simple key: value parser for flat configs

## Error Handling

- Missing config file is NOT an error (use defaults)
- Invalid YAML/JSON should warn but not fail the sweep
- Unknown keys should warn but be ignored
- Validate types (numbers are numbers, booleans are booleans)

## Related Beads

### 1. File Denylist Tests

```bash
test_denylist_exact_matches() {
    log_test_start "Exact denylist matches"
    
    local denied_files=(
        ".env"
        ".env.local"
        ".env.production"
        "config/.env"
        "id_rsa"
        "id_ed25519"
        ".git/config"
        "node_modules/package/index.js"
        "secrets.json"
        "credentials.yaml"
    )
    
    for file in "${denied_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing denylist for: $file"
        if \! is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly denied"
    done
}

test_denylist_glob_patterns() {
    log_test_start "Glob pattern matching"
    
    # Test *.pem pattern
    is_file_denied "server.pem" || fail "*.pem should match server.pem"
    is_file_denied "certs/client.pem" || fail "*.pem should match nested"
    
    # Test **/node_modules/** pattern
    is_file_denied "frontend/node_modules/pkg/a.js" || fail "node_modules nested"
    
    # Test .git/** pattern
    is_file_denied ".git/objects/abc123" || fail ".git internals"
}

test_denylist_allowed_files() {
    log_test_start "Allowed file verification"
    
    local allowed_files=(
        "src/main.py"
        "README.md"
        "config/settings.yaml"  # Not secrets.yaml
        ".gitignore"
        "package.json"
    )
    
    for file in "${allowed_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing allow for: $file"
        if is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be allowed but was denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly allowed"
    done
}
```

### 2. File Size Limit Tests

```bash
test_file_size_enforcement() {
    log_test_start "File size limit enforcement"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Create files of various sizes
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/small.bin" bs=1K count=100 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/at_limit.bin" bs=1M count=10 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/over_limit.bin" bs=1M count=11 2>/dev/null
    
    log_verbose "Testing 100KB file (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/small.bin" 10485760 && fail "100KB should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 10MB file at limit (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/at_limit.bin" 10485760 && fail "At limit should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 11MB file over limit (should fail)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/over_limit.bin" 10485760 || fail "Over limit should fail"
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "All size tests passed"
}
```

}

 {"id":"bd-701","title":"Implement parse_repo_url()","description":"**Parse all GitHub URL formats**

## What
Function to extract host, owner, and repo from various URL formats.

## Why
Users will provide URLs in many formats. We must handle them all correctly.

## Supported Formats
1. https://github.com/owner/repo
2. https://github.com/owner/repo.git
3. git@github.com:owner/repo.git
4. github.com/owner/repo
5. owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_url() {
    local url="$1"
    local -n _host=$2
    local -n _owner=$3
    local -n _repo=$4

    # Normalize: strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"

    if [[ "$url" =~ ^git@([^:]+):(.+)/(.+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^https?://([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="github.com"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        return 1
    fi
}
```

+# Check if value is a positive integer (>0)
+agent_sweep_is_positive_int() {
+    [[ "${1:-}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$1" -gt 0 ]]
+}
+
+# Set max file size in MB and keep bytes in sync
+set_agent_sweep_max_file_mb() {
+    local max_mb="${1:-}"
+    if agent_sweep_is_positive_int "$max_mb"; then
+        AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB="$max_mb"
+        AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_SIZE=$((max_mb * 1024 * 1024))
+        return 0
+    fi
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Set max file size in bytes and keep MB in sync (rounded up)
+set_agent_sweep_max_file_bytes() {
+    local max_bytes="${1:-}"
+    if agent_sweep_is_positive_int "$max_bytes"; then
+        AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_SIZE="$max_bytes"
+        AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB=$(((max_bytes + 1048575) / 1048576))
+        return 0
+    fi
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Apply max file size from config values (MB preferred, then bytes)
+apply_agent_sweep_max_file_limit() {
+    local raw_mb="${1:-}"
+    local raw_bytes="${2:-}"
+
+    if [[ -n "$raw_mb" && "$raw_mb" != "null" ]]; then
+        set_agent_sweep_max_file_mb "$raw_mb" || true
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$raw_bytes" && "$raw_bytes" != "null" ]]; then
+        set_agent_sweep_max_file_bytes "$raw_bytes" || true
+    fi
+}
+
+# Apply CLI override for max file size (MB)
+apply_agent_sweep_max_file_override() {
+    local override="${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB_OVERRIDE:-}"
+    [[ -z "$override" ]] && return 0
+    set_agent_sweep_max_file_mb "$override" || true
+}
+
+# Check if a file exceeds the max size (MB)
+# Usage: is_file_too_large /path/to/file [max_mb]
+# Returns: 0 if too large, 1 otherwise
+is_file_too_large() {
+    local file="${1:-}"
+    local max_mb="${2:-${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB:-0}}"
+    [[ -z "$file" || ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
+    agent_sweep_is_positive_int "$max_mb" || return 1
+
+    local max_bytes=$((max_mb * 1024 * 1024))
+    local size_bytes
+    size_bytes=$(stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
+    [[ "$size_bytes" -gt "$max_bytes" ]]
+}

+# Detect if a file is likely binary
+# Returns: 0 if binary, 1 otherwise
+is_binary_file() {
+    local file="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$file" || ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
+
+    if command -v file &>/dev/null; then
+        local info
+        info=$(file -b "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+        if echo "$info" | grep -qiE '(text|script|json|xml|empty|ascii|utf-8|unicode)'; then
+            return 1
+        fi
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Fallback: detect NUL bytes
+    if LC_ALL=C grep -q $'\x00' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+    return 1
+}

+# Check if a binary file is explicitly allowed by patterns
+# Usage: is_binary_allowed "path/to/file.bin"
+# Returns: 0 if allowed, 1 otherwise
+is_binary_allowed() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$file_path" ]] && return 1
+
+    file_path="${file_path#./}"
+    file_path="${file_path%/}"
+    local basename="${file_path##*/}"
+
+    local -a patterns=("${AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS_DEFAULT[@]}")
+    if [[ -n "${AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS:-}" ]]; then
+        read -ra extra <<<"$AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS"
+        patterns+=("${extra[@]}")
+    fi
+
+    local pattern
+    for pattern in "${patterns[@]}"; do
+        # shellcheck disable=SC2254
+        case "$file_path" in
+            $pattern) return 0 ;;
+        esac
+        # shellcheck disable=SC2254
+        case "$basename" in
+            $pattern) return 0 ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
